title: Estimating Uncertainty in Classifier Performance with Applications to Large Language Models and Nested Data
abstract: arXiv:2606.26422v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Researchers increasingly use text classification--supervised models or large language models--to measure constructs from natural language, providing metrics such as recall and precision as evidence of their validity. Yet, though these metrics are point estimates subject to sampling variation, measures of uncertainty are inconsistently reported alongside them. Further, when they are reported, they are often estimated with methods that are not appropriate when relevant labelled datasets are small or performance is high. To increase and improve confidence interval reporting in the field, this paper evaluates confidence interval methods for performance metrics under conditions typical of social science text classification: small to moderate sample sizes, infrequent constructs, and texts nested within individuals. Across simulations, default methods such as the Wald interval and the basic percentile bootstrap are the least accurate, with cove

translated_title: 估计分类器性能中的不确定性及其在大语言模型和嵌套数据中的应用
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26422v1 公告类型：新 摘要：研究人员越来越多地使用文本分类——包括监督模型或大语言模型——来从自然语言中测量概念，并提供召回率和精确率等指标作为其有效性的证据。然而，尽管这些指标是点估计值，会受到抽样变异的影响，但不确定性度量却很少与它们一起被一致地报告。此外，即使报告了这些不确定性度量，当相关标注数据集较小或模型性能较高时，通常使用的估计方法也不合适。为了在该领域增加并改进置信区间的报告，本文评估了在社会科学文本分类典型条件下——即样本量较小到中等、概念出现频率低、文本嵌套在个体中——性能指标的置信区间方法。在模拟中，Wald区间和基本百分位数自助法等默认方法是最不准确的，覆盖率表现不佳。
tldr: 本文评估了在社会科学文本分类典型条件下（小样本、低频概念、嵌套数据）的置信区间方法，发现Wald区间等默认方法最不准确，并提出了改进不确定性报告的建议。
en_tdlr: This paper evaluates confidence interval methods for text classification performance metrics under typical social science conditions (small samples, rare constructs, nested data), finding that default methods like the Wald interval are the least accurate, and offers recommendations for improving uncertainty reporting.
